title: Nahuatlea purpusii
intro: Nahuatlea purpusii, also known as the Purpus' nahuatlea, is a shrub native to Mexico that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nahuatlea purpusii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nahuatlea purpusii is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
